page: Amazon Web Services r6in.metal is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors. It features 128 vCPUs across 64 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 1024.0 GB of DDR4 RAM running at 3 GHz, providing a resource density of 8.0 GB of memory per physical core. It lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, relying entirely on network-attached storage. Network connectivity is a key feature, delivering a baseline bandwidth of 200 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong multi-core capabilities and high memory throughput. The server achieved a strong multi-core stress-ng score of 125,022.52 and a top-tier PassMark CPU score of 87,989.38. Memory bandwidth tests show strong results, reaching 338.95 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 133.36 GB/sec for large-block write operations. Cryptographic and vector processing performance is enhanced by hardware flags such as AVX-512 and AES, reflected in a top-tier PassMark CPU encryption test score of 114,667.56.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r6in.metal offers a high-density memory profile and high-speed networking on a bare-metal platform, eliminating hypervisor overhead. This makes it highly suitable for large-scale, memory-intensive workloads. It is optimized for large databases, in-memory caches like Redis, high-throughput web serving, and memory-bound enterprise applications.
description: Amazon Web Services r6in.metal is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server featuring 128 vCPUs from Intel Xeon 8375C processors running at 3.5 GHz. It is configured with 1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory, offering 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core, but contains no local storage or GPUs. Built for high-throughput networking, it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps. In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates strong multi-core performance with a PassMark CPU score of 87,989.38 and high memory bandwidth, reaching 338.95 GB/sec for large-block reads. It also shows strong database capabilities, executing 111.35 million Redis SET operations per second. This bare-metal instance is designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads, large-scale databases, in-memory caching, and high-bandwidth network applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6in.metal is a bare-metal server with 128 vCPUs, 1024 GB DDR4 RAM, and 200 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for memory-intensive databases and enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r6in.metal is a bare-metal server featuring 128 vCPUs, 1024 GB RAM, and 200 Gbps bandwidth for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A bare-metal, memory-optimized server featuring 128 vCPUs, one terabyte of RAM, and high-speed 200 Gbps baseline networking.
bullet_points: 128 vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors at 3.5 GHz
1024.0 GB of DDR4 system memory with 200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Bare-metal architecture with dedicated CPU allocation and no hypervisor overhead
Strong multi-core performance and high-throughput memory bandwidth
Best suited for large-scale databases, in-memory caching, and enterprise applications
